scenario: Stellar Insurance runs Camunda 8.8 with 5000 claims per day. While preparing a batch move of 200 claim instances, an underwriter requests a 2 hours pause to verify the target task. The operator wants to drop the in-progress selection in Operate without leaving the list view and without affecting any earlier operations.
question: Which actions allow the operator to discard the currently selected process instances while staying in batch modification mode?

options.text: Click the Discard option in the process instance toolbar, or clear the current selection to drop all currently selected instances from the batch.
Click Apply Modification once to commit the current selection, then use the browser back button to revert the batch back to an empty selection state.
Refresh the browser page; any in-progress batch selection in Operate is preserved server-side and is reset to empty on the next reload of the view.
Click the Exit button at the bottom of the list view, which both leaves batch mode and silently reapplies any prior modifications that were pending in the queue.

a.text: Correct. The docs say that in batch modification mode the operator can discard all currently selected instances either by clicking the Discard option in the process instance toolbar or by clearing the current selection.

b.text: Incorrect. Clicking Apply Modification commits the batch move; it does not provide an undo-by-back-button workflow, and the docs describe Discard or clearing the selection as the way to drop selections.

c.text: Incorrect. The docs do not describe in-progress selections as server-side preserved state, and refreshing the page is not the documented way to discard selections in batch mode.

d.text: Incorrect. The Exit button at the bottom of the list view leaves batch modification mode rather than reapplying pending modifications; it is not the documented discard mechanism for selections.

explanation: Inside batch modification mode the operator can discard all currently selected instances either by clicking the Discard option in the process instance toolbar or by clearing the current selection; the Exit button at the bottom of the list view leaves the modification mode entirely.
